title: MCP Audit
description: Comprehensive audit of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server implementation
executive_summary: The SnapBack MCP server implements a robust Model Context Protocol server with security measures, authentication, and integration with external services. It provides tools for risk analysis, dependency checking, and snapshot management. The implementation follows best practices for security, error handling, and performance.

security.authentication: API key based authentication with caching
security.input_validation: Zod schema validation for all inputs
security.path_validation: Strict file path validation to prevent traversal attacks

snapback.analyze_risk.description: Analyze code changes for potential risks before applying them
snapback.analyze_risk.implementation: Proxies to backend API with local fallback

snapback.check_dependencies.description: Check for dependency-related risks when package.json changes

snapback.create_checkpoint.description: Create a code checkpoint (snapshot) before making risky changes

snapback.list_checkpoints.description: List all available code checkpoints for restoration

snapback.restore_checkpoint.description: Restore code from a previously created checkpoint

catalog.list_tools.description: List available tools from connected external MCP servers
catalog.list_tools.implementation: Uses MCPClientManager to get tool catalog

ctx7.resolve-library-id.description: Resolve a library or package name into a Context7-compatible library ID

ctx7.get-library-docs.description: Fetch up-to-date documentation for a specific library

authentication.description: API key based authentication with caching

security.description: Security utilities for path validation and telemetry
features: File path validation to prevent traversal attacks

mcp_client_manager.description: Manager for connecting to external MCP servers

http_server.description: HTTP server wrapper for MCP with SSE support

performance.tracking: Performance tracking for operations with budgets

testing.unit_tests: Unit tests exist for core components
testing.integration_tests: Integration tests exist for MCP response processing

positive: Robust security implementation with path validation
Tier-based access control for Pro features
Good separation of concerns in implementation
concerns: Some WebSocket functionality has been removed for MVP
Some heavy objects are created on every startServer() call

recommendations: Increase test coverage to at least 80%
Consider re-implementing WebSocket transport for real-time communication
Implement lazy initialization for heavy objects to improve performance
Add more comprehensive security logging and monitoring
